basic_details.job_title: MPSeDC GET Program 3.0 2026 - Apply Online for 150 Graduate Engineer Trainee Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Madhya Pradesh State Electronics Development Corporation Ltd. (MPSeDC)
basic_details.post_name: Opportunity to appear in interviews for the position of Associate Engineer at MPSeDC (on payroll of empanelled Third-Party Agencies) for those who clear Half Yearly & Yearly Assessment
basic_details.short_information: MPSeDC is selecting top 150 candidates for a 1-year Graduate Engineer Trainee program with a monthly stipend of Rs. 25,000/-. Last date to apply is 16-Jun-2026. Examination is on 01-Jul-2026.

important_dates.document verification: After qualifying the online exam (date to be intimated by MPSeDC)
important_dates.date of joining (graduates): Within 15 days from date of issuance of Joining Letter
important_dates.date of joining (final semester candidat: Within 15 days from date of completion of final exams
important_dates.validity of merit list: Till next notification for examination or 6 months, whichever is earlier

application_fee.details: Payment Mode: Online through MP Online Portal (https://iforms.mponline.gov.in) or through MP Online Kiosk. Fee deposited will not be refunded under any circumstances. After payment, a one-time edit facility will be available to candidates for which a portal fee of Rs. 50/- + GST is applicable.

age_limit.details: No age relaxation details are mentioned in the notification for any specific category. Candidates are advised to check the official website for any updates on age relaxation.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions at the time of the notification of advertisement. Only Indian Nationals are eligible. Diploma or PG Diploma holders are not eligible. The degree-awarding date and the advertisement notification date must not be separated by more than 1 year (365 days).

selection_process: Selection is strictly based on performance in the online examination. There are no other means by which candidates would be onboarded for training.

Step 1 – Online Examination: Conducted by MP Online Ltd. on 01-Jul-2026. Consists of 85 MCQs + 15 Programming Questions. Duration: 3 hours (180 minutes). Minimum qualifying marks: 35%. Candidates scoring less than 35% shall be rejected. Step 2 – Merit List Preparation: Top 150 candidates based on examination score are selected. Remaining qualified candidates are kept in a waitlist of up to 50 candidates in sequential order of merit. Step 3 – Document Verification: Selected candidates undergo document verification at MPSeDC before joining. Documents including identity proof

and degree certificates are verified. Joining letter is issued only to those whose documents are found complete and in order. Step 4 – Joining: Candidates who have completed graduation must join within 15 days of the Joining Letter. Final semester candidates must join within 15 days of completion of final exams. No-show within 15 days leads to automatic nullification of candidature. Step 5 – Mid-Training Assessment: Performance reviewed at end of 6 months (Half Yearly Assessment) and end of 12 months (Yearly Assessment). Only those scoring above the threshold are retained. Others are disassociated after being issued a certificate. Certificate: Training certificate awarded only to candidates who complete at least 3 months. Candidates leaving before 6 months are NOT entitled to receive a certificate.

syllabus.details: Note: This syllabus is in accordance with the current work requirements of MPSeDC.

how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online only through the MP Online Portal. No hard copy applications will be accepted. Incomplete applications will be rejected.
Documents Required to be Uploaded with Online Application:
Latest passport-size photograph - not older than 6 months from the date of issue of advertisement
High School (10th Class) certificate - mandatory as age proof (no other document accepted for age)
Graduation/Post-Graduation degree certificate or marksheet from a recognized university - clear scanned and self-attested copy
For final semester candidates: document confirming enrollment in final semester; provisional degree/marksheet to be submitted within 2 months of joining

Before joining (document verification at MPSeDC): Self-attested copies of all educational qualifications, marksheets of entire year, and technical certificates must be presented for physical verification at MPSeDC, Bhopal.
important_instructions: Application must be submitted through MP Online Portal (www.mponline.gov.in) only - no other mode is accepted. Clear scanned and self-attested copies of all documents must be uploaded - blurry, tampered, or incomplete documents will lead to rejection. High School certificate is the only accepted proof of age - Aadhaar, PAN, or other documents will not be accepted as age proof. Candidates in final semester may apply but must submit their provisional degree/marksheet to MPSeDC within 2 months of joining - failure to do so results in cancellation of candidature. Any wrong or incomplete information furnished at any stage will lead to cancellation of candidature without notice. Candidates are liable for criminal prosecution for submitting false documents, impersonation, or use of unfair means. Candidates must sign an NDA with MPSeDC before joining - non-signing is grounds for cancellation of joining. Trainees must use their own laptop throughout the 1-year training period - no laptop will be provided. Only 1 Casual Leave per month is permitted; no leave encashment. LWP of maximum 3 days per month may be granted in exceptional cases. GETs who voluntarily exit must give 1 month notice period before exit. MPSeDC reserves the right to cancel or discontinue the training program at any point of time without assigning any reason. All disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of Courts of Bhopal. For latest updates, follow MPSeDC on social media: LinkedIn (mpsedc), Twitter (@MPSeDC_DST), Instagram (@mpsedc_dst), Facebook (MPSeDCDST), Telegram (mpsedcstateITcenter).

faq.question: Q1. What is MPSeDC GET Program 3.0 and is it a job or a training program?
faq.answer: MPSeDC GET Program 3.0 is a 1-year paid training program, not a permanent government job. Selected candidates join as trainees and receive a stipend of Rs. 25,000/- per month. Those who clear the Half Yearly and Yearly Assessments are issued a Certificate of Completion and get an opportunity to appear in interviews for the Associate Engineer position at MPSeDC on the payroll of empanelled Third-Party Agencies — it does not guarantee direct permanent employment.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply and what is the application fee?
faq.answer: The last date to apply is 16-Jun-2026. The application fee is Rs. 800/- (inclusive of GST), payable online through the MP Online Portal or through MP Online Kiosk. Fee is non-refundable under all circumstances. An edit facility is available post-payment for a one-time portal fee of Rs. 50/- + GST.

faq.question: Q3. I am a Diploma holder in Computer Science. Am I eligible?
faq.answer: No. Diploma or PG Diploma holders are explicitly excluded from the GET Program 3.0 regardless of their specialization, marks, or experience. Only candidates with a full B.E./B.Tech degree or Graduation/Post-Graduation in Computer Science/IT or equivalent are eligible.

faq.question: Q4. My college is in Madhya Pradesh but I belong to another state. Can I apply?
faq.answer: Yes. Both Domicile and Non-Domicile candidates are eligible, provided they have completed their qualifying degree from a Madhya Pradesh institution. State of domicile does not disqualify you — the MP degree requirement is the key condition.

faq.question: Q5. I graduated 14 months ago. Am I still eligible?
faq.answer: No. The gap between the award of your degree and the date of the MPSeDC advertisement notification must not exceed 365 days (1 year). If your degree was awarded more than 1 year before the advertisement date, you are ineligible for this program.

faq.question: Q6. What is the exam pattern and how should I prepare?
faq.answer: The exam consists of 85 MCQs + 15 Programming Questions in a 3-hour online format. The syllabus covers 6 areas: Java (20%), .Net (20%), PHP (20%), Quality Assurance (10%), Software Engineering (10%), and Emerging Technologies (20%). A minimum of 35% marks is required to qualify. Prepare all 6 areas proportionally and practice programming questions — especially in Java, PHP, and .Net frameworks — to score well enough to enter the top 150.

faq.question: Q7. Can I choose my exam center? What cities are available?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide 4 preferences from the proposed centers: Bhopal, Jabalpur, Indore, Gwalior, Sagar, Ujjain, and Satna. Duplicate choices are not acceptable. Final center allocation is at the discretion of MP Online Ltd. — your preference is considered but not guaranteed.

faq.question: Q8. I am in my final semester and haven't received my degree yet. Can I apply?
faq.answer: Yes. Final semester candidates may apply and appear in the examination. However, if selected, you must submit your provisional degree/marksheet to MPSeDC within 2 months of joining. If you fail to clear your final exams or cannot submit the document within the stipulated time, your candidature will be cancelled and the next waitlist candidate will be given the opportunity.
